mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 60 percent when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.0 percent upward.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive return probability above 55 percent over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days specifically during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of post-recovery periods or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions above trend levels signal robust economic activity and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above trend correlates with negative 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observations across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with inflation above 3.0 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL combination fails to predict negative returns with a probability exceeding 55 percent over 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below negative 0.5 percent signals supply chain contraction and manufacturing weakness, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO MoM changes are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales reflect immediate consumer spending strength, which translates directly into positive corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases fail to generate a positive return probability above 52 percent over 5-day horizons across rolling 3-year windows.
